topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up, a small, insistent chime on my phone screen. It was a direct message from Mr. Harrison. My breath hitched. I stared at the screen, my heart starting a frantic drum solo against my ribs. He had tagged me in a photo from the school picnic, a candid shot where I was laughing, something I hadn't realized he’d captured. A wave of warmth washed over me, quickly followed by a strange, unsettling prickle of awareness. I scrolled through his recent posts, noticing the subtle shifts in his usual academic tone—a shared interest in obscure historical documentaries, a quick, thoughtful comment on a philosophy thread I’d posted. It wasn't creepy, not exactly, but it felt like a bridge being silently built between our worlds, a connection I hadn't known existed. I felt a sudden urge to reach out, a hesitant curiosity bubbling up inside me.

[story 2]

I was meticulously organizing my desktop when I saw it: a profile picture of Mr. Davies, smiling warmly, and a recent status update about a challenging concept from our advanced physics class. My fingers froze over the keyboard. A slow, deep sigh escaped my lips. It wasn't shock that hit me first, but a profound sense of understanding. He was clearly passionate about the subject, sharing his enthusiasm with the world. It made me feel a strange sort of validation, as if my own dedication to physics wasn't just a solitary pursuit but part of a larger, shared intellectual landscape. I leaned back in my chair, the tension draining from my shoulders. I thought about how much he cared about seeing students engage, and a quiet sense of appreciation settled over me, making the digital intrusion feel less like an invasion and more like an acknowledgment.

[story 3]

The sheer absurdity of it hit me like a physical blow. I was doom-scrolling through Instagram when I spotted Mr. Evans’ story—a quick video of him struggling to assemble flat-pack furniture, captioned with a self-deprecating joke. My cheeks flushed hot. I immediately minimized the app, my hands trembling slightly. There was a flicker of something tender in my chest, a recognition of shared humanity beneath the professional veneer. He wasn't just an instructor; he was a person navigating his own life, sharing small, relatable moments. A soft, protective feeling bloomed in my stomach, a quiet desire to offer some kind of support, even from a distance. I took a deep, steadying breath, realizing this unexpected visibility just made him feel a little more real to me.
